audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's reversal from former honor to present humiliation: mockers, social collapse, breached defenses, divine silence, bodily anguish, and failed expectation of good/light.

verses.restored: But now those younger than I laugh at me, whose fathers I would have refused to set with the dogs of my flock.
What use to me was the strength of their hands, men whose vigor had perished?
With want and hunger they are gaunt, gnawing the dry ground in gloom and desolation.
They pluck salt herbs by the bushes, and broom roots are their food.
They are driven out from the midst; people shout after them as after a thief.
They dwell in dreadful ravines, in holes of the dust and rocks.
Among the bushes they bray; under nettles they huddle together.
Sons of fools, sons without name, they were whipped out of the land.
And now I have become their song; I am a byword to them.
They abhor me; they keep far from me and do not withhold spit from my face.
Because he has loosened my cord and humbled me, they have cast off restraint before me.
On my right hand the rabble rises; they push away my feet and build up against me their roads of ruin.
They break up my path; they help my calamity; they need no helper.
They come as through a wide breach; amid the crash they roll on.
Terrors are turned upon me; my dignity is chased like the wind, and my salvation passes like a cloud.
Now my soul is poured out within me; days of affliction seize me.
Night pierces my bones within me, and my gnawing pains do not lie down.
By great force my garment is disfigured; it binds me like the collar of my tunic.
He has thrown me into the mire, and I have become like dust and ashes.
I cry to you, but you do not answer me; I stand, and you only look at me.
You have turned cruel to me; with the strength of your hand you oppose me.
You lift me up to the wind; you make me ride it, and you dissolve me in the storm.
For I know you will return me to death, to the house appointed for all living.
Yet does not one stretch out a hand in ruin, and in calamity cry out for help?
Did I not weep for the one whose day was hard? Was not my soul grieved for the needy?
But when I hoped for good, harm came; when I waited for light, darkness came.
My inward parts boil and do not rest; days of affliction confront me.
I walk darkened, but not by the sun; I stand in the assembly and cry for help.
I have become a brother to jackals and a companion to daughters of the ostrich.
My skin turns black and falls from me, and my bones burn with heat.
My lyre has become mourning, and my flute the voice of weepers.
